## Discussion

A [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLVertexBufferLayout] object describes the layout of data in one of the vertex buffers of a of a [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLMesh] object.

For meshes whose vertex data is split into multiple vertex buffers (a *structure of arrays* design), the order of objects in this array reflects the order of buffers in the mesh’s [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLMesh/vertexBuffers] array, which in turn parallels the order of vertex attributes in the descriptor’s [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLVertexDescriptor/attributes] array. If a mesh contains interleaved data in a single vertex buffer (an *array of structures* design), the descriptor contains only one [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLVertexBufferLayout] object, which describes the offset between entries for consecutive vertices in the buffer.

# layouts

**Instance Property**

The list of vertex buffer layouts described by the vertex descriptor.

## Declaration

```swift
var layouts: NSMutableArray { get set }
```

## Discussion

A [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLVertexBufferLayout] object describes the layout of data in one of the vertex buffers of a of a [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLMesh] object.

For meshes whose vertex data is split into multiple vertex buffers (a *structure of arrays* design), the order of objects in this array reflects the order of buffers in the mesh’s [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLMesh/vertexBuffers] array, which in turn parallels the order of vertex attributes in the descriptor’s [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLVertexDescriptor/attributes] array. If a mesh contains interleaved data in a single vertex buffer (an *array of structures* design), the descriptor contains only one [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLVertexBufferLayout] object, which describes the offset between entries for consecutive vertices in the buffer.

## Working with Vertex Buffer Layouts

- **setPackedStrides()**: Sets the stride for each vertex layout to the minimum value to pack vertex data together in a single buffer.

items.description: Sets the stride for each vertex layout to the minimum value to pack vertex data together in a single buffer.
